item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This Is one of the most interesting SCPs I ever read. The progretion of the article is wonderfull, the documents are chilling and the end is creepy stuff. Good job man. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God damn, Anborough. Ketermeister strikes again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I translated this into thai articles

This gave me a thrills

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great… in hidsight of the supplements. I know this is one very particular criterion of mine (especially considering how freeform my rating standards tend to be), but I treat supplements as strictly optional.

As it is, it's OK but you really cannot make out what it is other than "there's a consciousness inside that thing".

No-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The second image is borrowed from: Neon Tommy, Annenberg Media Center

Original Source: http://www.neontommy.com/news/2011/10/german-satellite-hit-earth-weekend
Creator: NASA

item.title: Image Removal
item.content:encoded: SCP-1514's second image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. The first image is okay. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Live2Die or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent.

"Well, I believe we, the Americans of today, are ready to act worthy of ourselves, ready to do what must be done to ensure happiness and liberty for ourselves, our children, and our children's children. And as we renew ourselves here in our own land, we will be seen as having greater strength throughout the world. We will again be the exemplar of freedom and a beacon of hope for those who do not now have freedom."

- Ronald Reagan's Inaugural Address, January 20, 1981

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Few minor issues:

SCP-1514-1A's components are not to be tampered with, any action beyond routine inspection of the device is to be met with immediate termination.

Comma should be a semi-colon.

potassium-cyanide collar

Do you mean potassium cyanide (as in the compound KCN)? If so, it shouldn't be hyphenated, as the hyphen implies the collar contains both potassium and cyanide anions, but not the two mixed together. If this is your intention, fine. Also, note that to be fatal to humans, potassium cyanide must be eaten. So does the collar force it into their mouth or something?

subsequent investigation by the Unusual Incidents Unit (UIU), Federal Bureau of Investigation brought SCP-1514 to the Foundation's attention.

A second comma is needed after 'Investigation' in order to properly complete the subordinate clause.

Beyond that… well, maybe there were more errors that I didn't notice because I got caught up in the story. And what a story! Very good. I like it a lot. Scary and sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cold War, detailed references to covert government programs, fucked up experimentation, Akira-like undertones. This is fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though this isn't meant to destroy the whole world, it still makes me think of this:

Yes, but the whole point of the doomsday machine is lost if you keep it a secret! Why didn't you tell the world?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great writing, captivating and making you think, leaving place for various interpretations with the only right one. +1

Anborough, thanks for sharing with us what's really up with the SCP.

Hell, you had me at keter, xk and the pictures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whaaaaaaaaaa????!!!

Holy shit.

Somehow that revelation, which I did NOT pick up on, makes this about 10x more horrifying and wrong. And this SCP was already a healthy amount of horrifying/wrong to begin with under my previous assumptions.

EDIT: reread the article. Seems obvious now. I guess I just didn't want to consciously fathom something so horrible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Owl Thrower, we don't do the backronyms here, nor do we have a strict canon for K-Class scenarios. The Russian wiki has a canon for this, I believe — but it's not even one we can use, because it uses material removed from our wiki at the request of its author.

Voct: A few people have used "IK-Class collapse of global civilization scenario" for any civilization-destroying natural disaster (whether by anomalous or non-anomalous means), and XK for the rest ("salted earth") or occasionally Gears' GH/GK "dead greenhouse" scenario.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's UK (Unnaparent keter).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoiler
- Spoiler

There is only one fetus and mother, but while the fetus is intact (maybe), the mother isn't: the satellites all contain the replicated nervous tissue of the mother (that's why they're talking about replicating the sample).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… well. F***. You have managed to take a satellite-based laser array that could basically devastate half the world and induce the remaining half to blow itself to oblivion… and make it be made out of feels. That is not easy to do.

One thing I'm confused about though, the supplementary documents seem to indicate there was only one successful mother-fetus pair. What's the deal with all the -other- satellites then? Or did they make the woman have -more- terrifyingly powerful telepathic fetuses?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think "civilization-destroying nuclear war" should be considered an XK-class end-of-the-world scenario. An end-of-the-world scenario, yes, but this end-of-the-world scenario would be wholly non-anomalous, except for the trigger.

I've seen CK for "dominance shift" and "NK" for "gray goo" - how about "LK" for "non-anomalous end of the world triggered by anomalous means"? (another example: SCP-515 may be summoning the asteroids to Earth, but if they hit, there'll be nothing anomalous about the destruction they wreak.)

edit: that said, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooooh. I didn't make the-…

FUUUUUUUUUUUUUUUUUUCK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should probably give this a read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some observatories do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How many people look at satellites on an everyday basis? I think the answer is zero.

Or do amature astronomers look at satellites in detail? (I honestly don't know the answer to this.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …what do you mean kingdom-level above human?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The signal is telepathic. The Foundation calls it an anomalous transmission because they can't objectively verify that it is telepathic. Also, as stated in the second supplement, the fetus is required to control what's powering the satellites (lasers require an immense amount of energy).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but it has a pair of major flaws.

• Camouflage in space by making a satellite which looks like another satellite. It won't work very well. Anyone who sees it is probably more likely to be suspicious that there's an unexplained replica of the [REDACTED] satellite than if it looked completely different or like a piece of spacejunk. At least get rid of the solar panels considering how much every bit of wight costs to get into orbit.
• How exactly is this more effective than a mechanical equivalent because it uses a fetus? An automated "doomsday machine" computer could serve the same function as SCP-1514-1A in regards to controlling SCP-1514-2 assuming the control is by radio. Also, someone could find and replicate the signal to disarm SCP-1514-2. Might make more sense if the link was telepathic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually think the logs weren't quite as polished as the main article, but I don't care, because jesus christ. Take this upvote.

E: Just re-read and noticed again who the President was. Does that mean SCP-1514 is from a kingdom level above human?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm 100% serious. You even have two super-secret organizations fighting each other over how to best do it…SEELE vs NERV..

This is amazingly coincidental. It's truly astounding. You should be proud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh God you're not serious are you.

…I'm rather unsettled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dammit Anborough, you just keep poppin' out the hits, don't you?

I'm not entirely sure what's going on here, but there's enough to intrigue me and make me want more, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reread it. This is such an amazing SCP. It's a little reminiscent of F.E.A.R., which is not a bad thing at all. This is one of those SCPs where you just KNOW that some of the [REDACTED]s have to be something truly horrible.

I love the back story, the tone, and the underlying feeling of the Foundation just saying, "We don't know how the hell this thing works, but lets hope it keeps working."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm amazed it wasn't deliberate. Again, this in no way detracts from the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh it's worse than that. A woman being ripped from her child, and turned into a weapon? While her child watches? That's an Evangelion Unit. They're not robots. They're quasi-human things and the mechanical portions are restraints, so we might control them. But to make one, you need more than just a body. The body is just a vessel after all. You need a soul. Guess where that comes from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I'm still waiting for Meserach to write that up. >:I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After reading the NGE wiki's page on LCL, I can understand your thoughts, but the red liquid here is a life-support system, designed to keep the "components" alive without allowing them to develop.

Also I noticed that LCL is commonly stated to smell like blood, looking at first supplement (UIU report) and how the researcher smells it to make sure it's not blood…wow there a lot of parallels here. I'm a little creeped out because they're not intentional whatsoever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did this start life as the bunny in a satellite? Regardless, a beautiful article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, now that you mention it, this is pretty goddamn reminiscent of NGE. Anborough, that is in no possible way a bad thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So creepy. So wrong. So emotional. I will need to read this all again. For now, take a +1 you monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never watched Evangelion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the liquid that fills the satellite supposed to be LCL? I'm getting a real Evangelion vibe from this.

That aside, this was deliciously weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only thing I don't like about this is the "contingency-45-coronet" thing because not every procedure needs an awkward 3 part name like montauk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, I put a few Easter eggs in this one (but that's the only from Earthbound).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did you get some inspiration from giygas for the fetus's lines?

This is a very well done based-on-reality SCP :D +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty much everything I would want to say.

+ show block
– hide block

Something about putting a telepathic, volatile fetus in charge of missile defense systems is just shudder-inducingly creepy to me.

Fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MKULTRA or Montauk…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stunning. This is excellent. I really like the grounding in real world details, like Star Wars and MKULTRA (at least, I'm betting that's what you did there ^_^) It manages to be very creepy, while not being at all horror movie faire. The creep factor is in how it reads as plausible, but has such a brilliant darkness. This may wind up among my favorite SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the article itself enough to upvote.

The attached documents made it chilling.

The final addendum in the collapsible was heart-wrenching.

Wonderful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A long time ago, in a Galaxy Far, Far Away…

I gave you an upvote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: America's Dead Hand. I like, have an upvote.
